sfDiffs.newValue: 06/03 MF: Speak with Suley, Touker and Sam on the commercials offer expiring this month (sent via email)

updates.notes: Maissa actively advising against Adyen integration but agencies still including it in briefs. Influence being exerted but not yet fully landed with decision-makers.
updates.action: Ensure HMA and agency shortlist aligned on Shopify Payments in Tuesday meeting

updates.notes: Maissa confirmed rates expire April 1st. Three free months (Apr-Jun) offered if signed by end of March. Commercial terms clear and communicated to Michael.
updates.action: Ensure HMA leadership understand commercial deadline in Tuesday meeting

updates.notes: Target signature by end of March 2026 (before April 1 rate expiry). Michael acknowledged deadline and arranging Tuesday meeting with HMA.
updates.action: Track whether Tuesday HMA meeting results in agreement to sign by end of March

updates.notes: Timeline driven by Shopify commercial offer expiration April 1st. Maissa made explicit: rates expire end of month. Michael acknowledged and agreed it creates working deadline. Agency selection converging — Lime Sharp frontrunner at 180-200K, re-engaging Swanky.

New evidence: favorable commercial terms with free months, strong agency ecosystem, Michael engaged. Risks: agency cost concerns could stall, Adyen confusion persists, internal communication gaps on contract deadline.

narrativeUpdates.whyNow: The urgency has significantly sharpened. Shopify's favorable platform and payment rates, negotiated over six months ago, expire April 1, 2026. Maissa has secured three free months (April-June) if the contract is signed by end of March. Michael was previously unaware of this deadline but acknowledged it creates a concrete timeline to work toward. He is arranging a meeting with HMA leadership for Tuesday March 10 to discuss the commercial urgency and agency selection strategy. The agency evaluation is also converging — Lime Sharp is the frontrunner with a potential budget increase to £180-200K.
narrativeUpdates.supportNeeded:
- Maissa to present commercial deadline and free-month incentive directly to HMA leadership (Suley, Touker, Sam) in Tuesday March 10 meeting
- Continued reinforcement that Adyen is not a viable payment integration — agencies still including it in briefs
- Support Michael in agency negotiation/selection to prevent further delays
- Ensure Swanky re-engagement doesn't create additional delay to March contract signing
- Clarify flexibility on April 1 rate expiration if deal slips by days vs. weeks

newNextSteps.action: Michael to set up meeting with HMA and Maissa for Tuesday Mar 10 to discuss commercial deadline and agency selection
Maissa to speak with Suley, Touker, and Sam about expiring commercials offer
Michael to negotiate with Lime Sharp on cost and re-engage Swanky for final comparison
Ensure all shortlisted agencies remove Adyen from proposals and align on Shopify Payments
Target contract signature by end of March to lock in favorable rates and 3 free months

dealRisks.risk: Contract deadline awareness gap: Michael was unaware of April 1 rate expiration. HMA leadership may also be unaware.
dealRisks.mitigation: Tuesday meeting with HMA leadership is critical — Maissa must present deadline directly to economic buyers.

dealRisks.risk: Agency selection delay: Michael wants to re-engage Swanky AND negotiate with Lime Sharp, potentially extending beyond March deadline.
dealRisks.mitigation: Reinforce that agency selection and Shopify contract signing are separable. Accelerate agency decision.

dealRisks.risk: Adyen confusion persists in agency briefs despite repeated correction.
dealRisks.mitigation: Maissa to directly contact agencies with written guidance that Adyen is not available for their GMV tier.

dealRisks.risk: Loss of commercial terms: rates expire April 1, requiring renegotiation from scratch.
dealRisks.mitigation: Make Tuesday HMA meeting the forcing function. Articulate what changes if they miss the deadline.

newCalls.title: Shopify X Hawes & Curtis - agencies checkin

questions.q: Do we know the business reasons as to why they are migrating?
questions.notes: Strong evidence for payments migration: frustration with Adyen reserves and pricing. Platform migration reasons from commercetools less clear from available calls.
questions.solution: Confirm full business reasons for both platform and payments migration.
questions.action: Ask Trinny stakeholders to articulate top 3 business reasons for full platform migration beyond payments.

questions.q: Do we have specific quantifiable goals aligned with the project?
questions.notes: Payments cost gap (18.8% difference) and target reduction (~10%) quantified. £65M GPV known. Broader platform migration goals not documented.
questions.solution: Quantify expected benefits beyond payments: conversion rate uplift from Shop Pay, operational cost savings.
questions.action: Build value model showing total economic impact including Shop Pay conversion uplift on £65M GPV.

questions.q: Do they need to make a platform change to achieve this goal?
questions.notes: For payments, could theoretically switch from Adyen without changing platform. Platform migration appears separate but linked decision. Bundled Shopify + SP creates combined value.
questions.solution: Clarify whether Trinny views this as one integrated decision or two.
questions.action: Confirm with Mark/Hiten that platform and payments are evaluated as single decision.

questions.q: Do we understand the impact if this goal isn't achieved?
questions.notes: Mark's frustration with Adyen reserves implies significant cash flow impact. 18.8% cost differential on £65M GPV represents substantial ongoing overpayment.
questions.solution: Quantify cost of inaction: annual payments overspend staying on Adyen.
questions.action: Calculate and present annual cost of staying on Adyen vs moving to Shopify Payments.

questions.q: Is there a compelling event in place driving action?
questions.notes: Mark: 'critical path item for us is mobilizing right resources and we can't afford to mobilize until we've got to sign contracts.' Internal pressure to sign for November 2026 launch.

questions.q: Have I validated the metrics with the customer?
questions.notes: Mark provided 18.8% cost differential and 10% reduction target. Customer-validated metrics.

questions.q: Are the metrics compelling enough to justify change?
questions.notes: Mark ready to sign if rates meet halfway. Combination of Adyen frustration, quantified cost gap, and readiness to sign Monday demonstrates compelling metrics.

questions.q: Have I identified the true economic buyer?
questions.notes: Mark is clearly the economic buyer. 'I don't need to go through any other approval processes. I can sign Monday.'

questions.q: Have I personally engaged with the economic buyer?
questions.notes: Mark directly participated in March 6 call with Maissa and Shiv. Deep engagement on commercial terms.

questions.q: Does the economic buyer have the authority and budget?
questions.notes: Mark explicitly confirmed: 'I don't need to go through any other approval processes.' Both authority and budget confirmed.

questions.q: Does the economic buyer support this initiative?
questions.notes: Mark actively driving the deal, proposing to sign Monday, expressing strong dissatisfaction with Adyen.

questions.q: Have I validated the budget and decision authority?
questions.notes: Mark validated authority ('I can sign Monday afternoon') and budget parameters (need ~10% rate reduction).

questions.q: Is the economic buyer accessible for key decisions?
questions.notes: Mark highly accessible — responsive, proposed Monday turnaround.

questions.q: Do we understand the full decision-making process?
questions.notes: Decision process clear: Mark evaluates commercial terms, aligns rates, signs. No committee.

questions.q: Have the decision steps been validated with the champion?
questions.notes: Mark laid out: (1) Shopify provides revised rates Monday morning, (2) Mark reviews, (3) signs Monday afternoon.

questions.q: Have we identified all stakeholders in the process?
questions.notes: Mark is clear commercial decision-maker. Hiten engaged on technical side. Full stakeholder map incomplete for deal of this size.
questions.solution: Confirm whether other stakeholders need alignment.
questions.action: Ask Mark if anyone else needs to be informed or consulted.

questions.q: Do we know the timeline for the decision?
questions.notes: Mark proposed signing Monday March 9. SF confirms: 'Looking to sign on March 9th.'

questions.q: Are there any legal/procurement/compliance steps?
questions.notes: SF mentions awaiting Shopify legal responses. Unclear if Trinny has own legal review requirement.
questions.solution: Clarify Trinny legal review requirements.
questions.action: Confirm with Mark whether Trinny legal review is needed.

questions.q: Is there a formal evaluation or RFP process?
questions.notes: No formal RFP. Direct commercial negotiation.

questions.q: Have we mapped the decision process in writing?
questions.notes: SF captures immediate process but no formal MAP or documented process beyond next steps.
questions.solution: Document end-to-end process from signing through implementation.
questions.action: Create mutual action plan covering signing to November launch.

questions.q: Do we know all the criteria the customer will use?
questions.notes: Primary: payments cost parity. Secondary: reserve transparency, Klarna/BNPL, platform capabilities.
questions.solution: Compile complete criteria list.
questions.action: Validate full decision criteria beyond payments rates.

questions.q: Can we influence or shape the decision criteria?
questions.notes: Shiv introduced Shop Pay conversion benefits (66% UK adoption) and Klarna direct contracting. Actively reframing total value.

questions.q: Is our solution aligned with the decision criteria?
questions.notes: Aligned on platform, POS (19 terminals), payments innovation. 18.8% payments cost gap remains.
questions.solution: Secure additional rate reduction.
questions.action: Maissa to finalize rate proposal meeting Mark's ~10% reduction threshold.

questions.q: Have we validated the criteria with the customer?
questions.notes: Mark stated: 'get combined rate down by 10%... reasonably confident math works.' Customer-validated.

questions.q: Do we know the relative weighting of each criterion?
questions.notes: Payments cost is #1 gating criterion. Reserve transparency #2. Platform capabilities satisfied.
questions.solution: Confirm rate alignment is the final blocker.
questions.action: Confirm with Mark that no other criteria are outstanding.

questions.q: Are there any criteria where we are weak?
questions.notes: 18.8% more expensive than Adyen on blended basis. Primary blocker.
questions.solution: Rate reductions + Shop Pay conversion uplift as offsetting value.
questions.action: Prepare total cost of ownership model including conversion uplift.

questions.q: Can we differentiate ourselves on these criteria?
questions.notes: Differentiation through Shop Pay (66% UK adoption), unified platform, transparent reserves vs Adyen. Rate disadvantage needs total value framing.
questions.solution: Build compelling total value narrative.
questions.action: Include conversion uplift, reserve transparency, operational simplification in proposal.

questions.q: Do we understand the contract/procurement process?
questions.notes: Mark: 'I don't need any other approval processes. As soon as you come back and we align, I can sign Monday.'

questions.q: Have we engaged with legal/procurement teams?
questions.notes: SF mentions awaiting Shopify legal. Unclear if Trinny needs own legal review.
questions.solution: Confirm Trinny legal requirements.
questions.action: Ask Mark if Trinny legal needs to review.

questions.q: Are there any known contract blockers?
questions.notes: Two blockers: (1) Payments rate alignment — need ~10% reduction. (2) Reserve calculation question outstanding.
questions.solution: Get rates team approval and prepare reserve documentation.
questions.action: Maissa to secure final rates and prepare reserve calculation explanation.

questions.q: Is there a redline process and expected timeline?
questions.notes: Mark implied no redline on his side. Shopify legal response pending.
questions.solution: Ensure Shopify legal clears before Monday.
questions.action: Follow up with Shopify legal for contract clearance.

questions.q: Do we have executive sponsorship to expedite?
questions.notes: Mark is exec sponsor on Trinny side. Unclear if Shopify side has exec sponsorship for rate/legal expediting.
questions.solution: Escalate rate request internally.
questions.action: Shiv/Maissa to escalate rate approval through leadership if needed.

questions.q: Have we identified all signatories?
questions.notes: Mark is sole signatory. No additional approvals needed.

questions.q: Is there a target signature date?
questions.notes: Target: Monday March 9, 2026. Mark proposed this directly.

questions.q: Have we identified the primary business pain?
questions.notes: Dual pain: (1) Adyen reserve practices ('crooks, hold on to a shitload of our money'), (2) 18.8% payments cost gap.

questions.q: Is the pain acknowledged by the customer?
questions.notes: Mark extremely vocal — 'crooks,' 'shitload of our money,' 'makes no sense whatsoever.' Deeply felt exec-level pain.

questions.q: Does the pain have financial or operational impact?
questions.notes: 18.8% cost differential on £65M GPV is substantial. Reserve hold-up ties up significant working capital.

questions.q: Is the pain urgent enough to drive action?
questions.notes: Mark wants to sign Monday. 'Can't afford to mobilize resources until we sign contracts.' November 2026 launch creates hard timeline.

questions.q: Can our solution directly address the pain?
questions.notes: Shopify Payments directly replaces Adyen. Shopify platform replaces commercetools. Shop Pay adds conversion value. POS (19 terminals).

questions.q: Have we validated the pain with multiple stakeholders?
questions.notes: Pain deeply validated with Mark. Hiten engaged on technical side. Limited evidence across other stakeholders.
questions.solution: Identify additional stakeholders with pain points.
questions.action: Map additional stakeholders and their specific pain points.

questions.q: Is the pain connected to strategic business objectives?
questions.notes: Resource mobilization urgency and November launch suggest strategic initiative. Explicit connection to broader strategy not articulated.
questions.solution: Connect migration to Trinny's broader strategic goals.
questions.action: Map implementation to strategic business objectives post-signing.

questions.q: Is the customer actively seeking to resolve the pain?
questions.notes: Trinny actively driving resolution. Mark proposed Monday signing timeline. Deep engagement on both technical and commercial workstreams.

questions.q: Have we identified a champion within the organisation?
questions.notes: Mark is both champion and economic buyer. Actively driving the deal. Hiten is secondary champion on technical side.

questions.q: Does the champion have influence with the economic buyer?
questions.notes: Mark IS the economic buyer with unilateral signing authority.

questions.q: Does the champion actively advocate for our solution?
questions.notes: Mark actively working to make deal happen. Provided exact rate reduction needed, proposed signing Monday.

questions.q: Can the champion navigate internal politics?
questions.notes: Mark stated no additional approvals needed. He owns the decision.

questions.q: Have we provided the champion with tools to sell internally?
questions.notes: Maissa preparing fee comparison visual. Feb 17 SE follow-up covered technical questions. Main 'tool' Mark needs is the right commercial proposal.
questions.solution: Provide clean commercial summary.
questions.action: Include one-page commercial summary with contract package.

questions.q: Does the champion have a personal win tied to this?
questions.notes: Mark's frustration with Adyen is personal. Moving off Adyen would be clear operational win. Specific personal motivations beyond cost savings not documented.
questions.solution: Understand Mark's broader objectives.
questions.action: Explore Mark's personal success metrics in future interactions.

questions.q: Is the champion relationship at risk?
questions.notes: Relationship strong but deal 'Committed - At Risk.' If rates can't be met, risk of losing both deal and relationship.
questions.solution: Meet commercial expectations or present compelling alternative value.
questions.action: Ensure Monday proposal meets 10% target or presents compelling total-value case.

questions.q: Do we know who the competitors are?
questions.notes: SF lists commercetools (current platform). Adyen is current PSP. Unclear if evaluating other platforms/PSPs.
questions.solution: Confirm if evaluating alternatives.
questions.action: Ask Mark: 'Are you evaluating any other options alongside Shopify?'

questions.q: Do we understand their strengths and weaknesses?
questions.notes: Adyen weakness: opaque reserves. Adyen strength: lower rates (18.8% cheaper). Commercetools weaknesses implied by migration decision.
questions.solution: Document what Trinny values about current setup.
questions.action: Review SE notes for gaps between commercetools and Shopify.

questions.q: Are we positioned favourably against the competition?
questions.notes: Favorable on platform (they chose Shopify) and payments experience. Unfavorable on pricing (18.8% gap).
questions.solution: Close rate gap.
questions.action: Secure rate approval meeting Mark's threshold.

questions.q: Have we developed a competitive strategy?
questions.notes: Clear strategy: reduce rates, frame total value with Shop Pay, Klarna direct option, emphasize reserve transparency.

questions.q: Is the customer aware of our competitive advantages?
questions.notes: Shiv communicated Shop Pay stats (66% UK). Reserve transparency implied. Total value narrative not yet formally presented.
questions.solution: Present comprehensive competitive advantage case.
questions.action: Include Shop Pay data, reserve comparison, total value model in Monday proposal.

narrative.oppSummary: Trinny London is a £82,800 MCV Deal Craft opportunity (£4.5M total 3-year revenue) with £65M Payments GPV migrating from commercetools + Adyen to Shopify Plus + Shopify Payments. Mark (commercial decision-maker) has unilateral signing authority and proposed signing Monday March 9 if payments rates align. The 18.8% blended cost gap between Shopify Payments and Adyen is the primary blocker — Mark needs ~10% reduction to make the math work. Proposed launch November 2, 2026 with 19 POS terminals in scope.
narrative.whyChange: Trinny London is deeply frustrated with Adyen's payment practices. Mark described their reserve calculation as exploitative: 'Adyen are almost crooks. They use it as a way to hold on to a shitload of our money which makes no sense whatsoever.' The 18.8% total cost difference between Shopify Payments and Adyen is quantified and driving the commercial negotiation. Beyond payments, the platform migration from commercetools suggests broader architectural needs — SE notes reference comprehensive discussions around store architecture, dual-store implications, Markets guidance, and tax considerations.
narrative.whyShopify: Shopify offers a unified platform plus payments solution consolidating Trinny's tech stack. Shiv highlighted 66% UK Shop Pay registration/active usage, positioning conversion benefits as a value driver beyond pure rate comparison. Creative solutions like Klarna direct contracting demonstrate flexibility. Comprehensive SE engagement (Feb 17 follow-up covering store architecture, Markets, tax) shows Shopify can address complex multi-market requirements. POS with 19 terminals addresses omnichannel needs.
narrative.whyNow: Mark stated urgency clearly: 'the critical path item for us is mobilizing the right resources and we can't afford to mobilize the resources until we've got to sign contracts.' He proposed signing Monday March 9. SF confirms: 'Looking to sign on March 9th.' November 2, 2026 launch date creates a hard implementation timeline driving urgency backward. Merchant intent is 'Committed - At Risk' — they want to move forward but the payments rate gap is the remaining blocker.
narrative.supportNeeded:
- Payments rate reduction: Mark needs ~10% blended reduction (domestic 0.95%+25p confirmed; international still needed)
- Reserve calculation clarity: outstanding question on how Shopify calculates reserves
- Legal team response: contract terms need finalizing for Monday signing
- Klarna rate optimization: explore direct contracting for better BNPL rates
- Conversion rate comparison visual: fee comparison showing total cost of ownership including Shop Pay benefits

newNextSteps.action: Secure final rate approval from internal rates team for ~10% blended reduction
Get Shopify legal team clearance on contract terms
Prepare reserve calculation explanation for Mark
Build and present fee comparison visual showing total cost of ownership
Send final commercial proposal to Mark Monday morning for Monday afternoon signature
Explore Klarna direct contracting option for Trinny to reduce BNPL costs

dealRisks.risk: Payments rate gap may not close: Mark needs ~10% blended reduction and current 5-bip domestic movement may be insufficient
dealRisks.mitigation: Combine rate reductions with total value narrative. Escalate rate request internally. Present Klarna direct option.

dealRisks.risk: Shopify legal may not respond in time for Monday March 9 signing target
dealRisks.mitigation: Escalate legal review urgency. Flag Monday signing commitment to legal immediately.

dealRisks.risk: Reserve calculation question: if Shopify's approach perceived like Adyen's, could derail deal given Mark's extreme sensitivity
dealRisks.mitigation: Prepare clear, transparent documentation. Emphasize differences from Adyen's approach.

dealRisks.risk: SE coverage gap during Stephen's paternity leave (Feb 18 - Mar 17)
dealRisks.mitigation: Ensure Ben Homer is briefed on all outstanding Trinny technical items.

dealRisks.risk: Merchant 'Committed - At Risk': if Monday signing slips, momentum could fade
dealRisks.mitigation: Treat Monday deadline as hard. If delays, communicate transparently with Mark.

newCalls.title: Shopify X Trinny - commercials
newCalls.summary: Commercial negotiation focused on closing payments rate gap. Mark calculates 18.8% total cost difference with Adyen. Needs ~10% reduction to make math work. Proposed signing Monday March 9. Two blockers: rate alignment and reserve calculation question. Shopify offered domestic rate reduction to 0.95%+25p. Shiv introduced Shop Pay conversion benefits and Klarna direct option.
